_note: Limitation / action-window table that drives the red/amber/green signal (arch doc Section 4). Encoding deadlines as DATA is what turns the LED from decoration into the most valuable thing the box does. EVERY period here is a STARTING REFERENCE and must be verified against current law before the demo (see the caveat at the top of the architecture doc). 'limitation_days' = the hard legal deadline; 'recommended_action_days' = the advisory 'act within' window we surface on the OLED; 'signal' = base colour for this matter type.

wages.limitation_basis: Code on Wages, 2019, s.45 — an application for a claim relating to wages must generally be filed within three years from the date on which the amount became due. (VERIFY.)
wages.explanation: Wages already earned are legally due. You have up to about three years to file a claim, but acting soon matters: evidence (attendance, messages, witnesses) fades and the money is already owed to you.

legal_notice_reply.limitation_basis: As stated in the notice itself — often 15 to 30 days. (VERIFY against the specific notice.)
legal_notice_reply.explanation: A legal notice sets its own reply deadline. Missing it can be treated as ignoring the matter.

consumer.limitation_basis: Consumer Protection Act, 2019 — generally 2 years from the cause of action. (VERIFY.)
consumer.explanation: Consumer complaints generally must be filed within two years of the problem arising.

cheque_bounce.limitation_basis: NI Act s.138 — 15-day statutory notice period + 30 days to file after that. (VERIFY.)
cheque_bounce.explanation: A cheque-bounce action runs on strict day-counts; missing them can end the case.

general.limitation_basis: No fixed deadline for simply knowing your rights.
general.explanation: This is general awareness — no clock is running.
